They make two Strikes against their hunted prey, one with each of the required weapons. If both hit the same hunted prey, combine their damage for the purpose of its resistances and weaknesses. The Isgeri Slayer applies their multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally.
BwbytHd1FLfsLS5h.name: Twin Takedown

gvyo7DCeFHOxTwMo.description:
The Isgeri Slayer designates a single creature they can see and hear, or one they're Tracking, as their prey.

The slayer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [[/act seek]] the prey and to Survival checks to [[/act track]] the prey.

The first time the slayer hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d8 precision damage. The slayer also ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment. These effects last until the poacher uses Hunt Prey again.
gvyo7DCeFHOxTwMo.name: Hunt Prey

2WkTyHMdIQK8yDq4.description: When Jordis has their shield raised, they can Shield Block when an attack is made against an adjacent ally. If they do, the shield prevents that ally from taking damage instead of Jordis.
2WkTyHMdIQK8yDq4.name: Shield Warden

bo3McL0Zd968yq7C.description: When Jordis rolls a success on a Will save against a fear effect, they get a critical success instead. Any time they gain the frightened condition, reduce its value by 1.
bo3McL0Zd968yq7C.name: Bravery

dsTEbJP8gKHXqObZ.description:
Requirements Jordis has their shield raised

Effect Jordis presses forward, using their shield to push back foes. Jordis Strides and Shoves, in either order. The multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove}, though the Shove does count toward Jordis's multiple attack penalty.
dsTEbJP8gKHXqObZ.name: Shielded Advance

Kylix.description:
The elegant and mortal-appearing kylixes, or cupbearer devils, rarely hide their infernal nature, preferring to dress tastefully and immaculately to accentuate the beauty and symmetry of their forms. Theirs is the dance of idle speech and clever chatter, half-promises of friendship amid smiling hints of dalliance. They make friends swiftly wherever they go, and their presence alone seems to prevent insurrection and conflict. The cupbearers are the face of occupation; with their devilish charms, they ca

The cupbearers shape art and culture, wielding their talents in hospitality and merrymaking to seduce their guests and entourages toward the glamour of evil. Hell is, after all, a bastion of civilization; the cupbearers, with their twinkling vessels and carelessly sincere flattery, remind that Hell is the best place to be, and you have nothing to fear from its laws.

LR1T4lgpq4Zds9zm.description: When a kylix is holding a drink and not wielding weapons or wearing armor, they're continually protected by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.8xRzLhwGL7Dgy3EZ]{Sanctuary} spell (@Check[will|dc:21]). If the kylix breaks the sanctuary spell, the effect returns if the devil doesn't take any hostile actions for 10 minutes. Additionally, while protected by a sanctuary spell, the kylix can roll Diplomacy for initiative.
LR1T4lgpq4Zds9zm.name: Raise a Glass

dZhdS0NIUFbQffp2.description: The kylix attempts a @Check[deception], @Check[diplomacy], @Check[performance], or @Check[society] check, and compares their result against the Will DCs of up to four creatures within 60 feet. On a success, these creatures are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} for 1 round. The kylix can Sustain this effect, increasing the duration of this fascination by 1 round for up to 1 minute. When the fascination ends, the affected creatures are then temporarily immune to Courteous Causerie for 1 hour.
dZhdS0NIUFbQffp2.name: Courteous Causerie

nOv18QCiDQQq1zPa.description: The kylix can flank an enemy if it's within reach of both an ally and themself; the kylix and their ally don't have to be on opposite sides. This benefits the kylix's allies as well, but only if they're flanking with the kylix, not each other. Other requirements for flanking must still be met.
nOv18QCiDQQq1zPa.name: Hand in Glove

wgN1i967ydERLWxG.description: The kylix's non-damaging spells gain the subtle trait.
wgN1i967ydERLWxG.name: False Friend

zEv2kbEnkCr8olYy.description: A creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by a kylix is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the kylix.
zEv2kbEnkCr8olYy.name: Sparkling Wit

Kylix.name: Kylix
Kylix.npcAllSaves: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

FLpNSzIq0TlBKJ1b.description: 60 feet. Lefranek's false flag creates panic and confusion. Hellbreakers in the area take a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws and DCs vs. effects that give the frightened or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} condition. Add 1d8 to the Gala Crowd hazard's check to determine its action during its routine.
FLpNSzIq0TlBKJ1b.name: False Flag

oOE6UU4R12ilWWnP.description:
Lefranek condemns an enemy within 30 feet as a Chelaxian dog and enemy of Isger. Until the start of Lefranek's next turn, any Strikes she or her allies make against the creature do an additional 1d4 precision damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.izuJvgHjqI6kQNbK]{Effect: Denounce}
oOE6UU4R12ilWWnP.name: Denounce

Lights Out.description: Patches of darkness envelop the rickety beds in this room, from which can be heard the sounds of gentle snoring from children.
Lights Out.hazardDisable: @Check[occultism|dc:18] (trained) to ward off the darkness or @Check[deception|dc:19] or @Check[performance|dc:19] (trained) to pretend to be asleep; two total success are required to disable the haunt
Lights Out.hazardReset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the room but resets immediately thereafter. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer here is destroyed.
Lights Out.hazardRoutine: (1 action; darkness, occult) The haunt randomly chooses 1 living creature that it hadn't engulfed in darkness the previous round. Supernatural darkness wells over that creature, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. On a failure, that creature is engulfed in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] of darkness centered on them and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round. Any creatures in the emanation must attempt their own Will save or become blinded as well. Any non-magical light sources and magical light of 1st rank or lower the primary creature is holding or wearing cease to give off light for that duration.
Lights Out.name: Lights Out

Littler Beasts.description: At first, the tree looks small. Then the space around it warps to give the impression that it's a normalsized tree seen at a distance.
Littler Beasts.hazardDisable: @Check[nature|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] twice to disperse the warped magic around the tree, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (2nd rank; counteract DC 19) to counteract the warped magic
Littler Beasts.hazardReset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures within 60 feet of it at the start of its turn.
Littler Beasts.hazardRoutine: (1 action) The trap targets a random creature within 60 feet to try to bring it down to its size. That creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. On a failure, the creature becomes Tiny in size until the beginning of its next turn. An affected creature's equipment shrinks with it but returns to its original size if removed. The creature's reach changes to 0 feet. This is a polymorph effect that has no effect on a Tiny creature. On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for the same duration.
Littler Beasts.name: Littler Beasts

PUgNVko9y7jrqzRb.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Nayren's strikes keeps them empowered to continue to take down rebels. For 1 minute or until they critically miss, the first time each round Nayren successfully Strikes a creature, they gain 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round.
PUgNVko9y7jrqzRb.name: Rack the Dissidents

QiDBXiAnEA0qfHl4.description: Whenever Nayren succeeds on a save against an illusion effect, they get a critical success instead.
QiDBXiAnEA0qfHl4.name: Disillusionment

TKOBWw9Pzld5ZYd1.description: Nayren makes a longsword Strike. If the attack hits, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save or be unable to use auditory actions for 1 round. This also prevents the target from casting spells, with the exception of subtle spells or spells that don't require the use of magical words in casting. On a critical failure, the duration is instead 1 minute. Regardless of the outcome of the save, the target becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 10 minutes.
TKOBWw9Pzld5ZYd1.name: Silence, Heretic!

tlkGe5uDBgQGh5ZO.name: +3 Status to Reflex Saves vs. Area Effects

zILtmhcHRhMV5YrB.description:
Trigger Nayren reaches 0 Hit Points or fewer

Effect The battle within the nephilim's blood runs on. Nayren automatically stabilizes and gains 1 Hit Point. They Stand. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.
zILtmhcHRhMV5YrB.name: Return to Battle

AIx6uVBQxkYJceYV.description: The novitiate makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.
AIx6uVBQxkYJceYV.name: Flurry of Blows

Odvolos.description: As empires posture amid decline and demagogues preside over republics, censorship devils scatter the burning ash of ignorance as a comforting veil over the complications of history and causality. These devils are feverishly devoted to Hell and find anything that could be interpreted against Hell's doctrines to be anathema and deserving of immediate destruction. Inconvenient accounts of the past, truths that draw the faithful away from the ways of Asmodeus, words that bring hope to those who might otherwise fall into a devil's temptations—these are fuel for the odvolos's hateful torches, burning bright on the cinders of ink and vellum.

2QiHYHOogtqH19lt.name: -1 Status to All Saves vs. Emotion Effects

BjycFDoNB3L064aX.description:
Trigger A creature the odvolos can see or hear within 15 feet Casts a Spell from a scroll or Activates a grimoire

Effect The odvolos Steps toward the creature and makes a longspear Strike. If the attack is a critical hit, the triggering action is disrupted.
BjycFDoNB3L064aX.name: Purge Records

KoMfzVvaYXwnhc0G.description: An odvolos ignores @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} or penalties to Perception from ash, fire, and smoke.
KoMfzVvaYXwnhc0G.name: Fevered Consciousness

STeGuJ5028k9wlaF.description:
The odvolos attempts to [[/act demoralize]] a creature within 30 feet. If the odvolos succeeds, the creature also gains fire weakness 5 while frightened.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.u3Qy9t7RuE4wKV0O]{Effect: Pyre's Mercy}
STeGuJ5028k9wlaF.name: Pyre's Mercy

hC9xiB50R6GDryIv.description: Piercing weapons wielded by an odvolos gain the magical trait and deal an additional 1d4 dire damage on a successful Strike and 1d8 persistent fire damage on a critical hit.
hC9xiB50R6GDryIv.name: Torchbearer

kWqJyo2oTEPHTCr4.name: Locate (At Will, Books, Scrolls, Ink, Writing Materials, and Other Written or Inscribed Materials Only)

vvBpkNSv6mOdSUVi.description: 15 feet. The odvolos is surrounded by an aura of burning paper and scorched parchment, which provides concealment as if it was smoke. A creature within the aura takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks, and a creature that ends its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save or any items with written information held or carried by the creature takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]] damage. Magical items and religious texts are immune to this effect.
vvBpkNSv6mOdSUVi.name: Shroud of Burning Pages

Odvolos.name: Odvolos
Odvolos.npcAllSaves: +1 status to all save vs. magic, -1 to all saves vs. emotion effects

0zdqXeljScyRv0K3.description:
Requirements Phoebe uses Commander's Orders

Effect Phoebe grants one ally in her Hellbreakers League banner aura an extra reaction that they can use only to follow her Commander's Orders. If they don't use the reaction, it's lost.
0zdqXeljScyRv0K3.name: Trained Reaction

2gR12XiZvj6T9iNV.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Phoebe calls out one of the following orders to all her allies in her Hellbreakers League banner aura, letting them immediately take the associated action as a reaction.

  • On Guard! The ally can Raise a Shield, defensively position a weapon with the parry trait, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ust1jJSCZQUhBZIz]{Take Cover}. If the ally isn't benefiting from cover or near a feature that allows them to Take Cover, they can fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} as part of the reaction.
  • Send Them Back to Hell An ally who's flanking a devil, a creature that worships devils, or a creature that has a magical contract with a devil can immediately attempt a Strike.
  • To Me! The ally can Stride but must end their movement in the aura. The ally can Burrow, Climb, Fly, or Swim instead of Striding if they have the corresponding movement type.
2gR12XiZvj6T9iNV.name: Commander's Orders

MifWbFnP6jYyZIgk.description: 30 feet. As Phoebe displays the Hellbreakers League banner, all allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to saves and DCs against devils and Hellknights.
MifWbFnP6jYyZIgk.name: Banner Bearer

W5gmc4myKrHAJx7D.description: Phoebe can use Warfare Lore to Recall Knowledge about most creatures regardless of type, but typically only to determine whether they can be reasoned with, their most notable offensive abilities, and whether one of their saving throws is particularly weak.
W5gmc4myKrHAJx7D.name: Warfare Expertise

wZ7TPHEB6kKzU7sk.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Phoebe learns Alceo's present state. She knows his direction and distance, and any conditions affecting him.
wZ7TPHEB6kKzU7sk.name: Sense Heartbond

Play Time.description: An old, dusty toy box creaks open and forcefully expels a series of cracked and broken toys.
Play Time.hazardDisable: @Check[occultism|dc:19] (trained) to exorcise the spirits of the toy box, @Check[athletics|dc:20] (trained) to hold the toy box closed momentarily, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:21] (trained) to convince the spirits that play time is over; three total success are required to disable the haunt
Play Time.hazardReset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the room or if the toy box is destroyed. After 1 hour, if the toy box remains, the haunt reactivates. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer in area D5 is destroyed.
Play Time.hazardRoutine: (1 action) The toy box belches forth a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of toys, dealing 3d8 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (chosen randomly each time) with a @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save.
Play Time.name: Play Time

Practice Guard's Grasp.description: A spring-loaded net fires from the dummy, then is pulled tightly back in with magnets.
Practice Guard's Grasp.hazardDisable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to jam the net or @Check[athletics|dc:25] to knock the dummy over
Practice Guard's Grasp.name: Practice Guard's Grasp

Prison Break.description: A prison mob makes a break for freedom, trampling anything in its path.
Prison Break.hazardDisable: @Check[athletics|dc:27] to stop the prisoners in their tracks, @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] to reason with the mob, or @Check[intimidation|dc:27] to cow them into calming down
Prison Break.hazardRoutine:
(1 action) Prisoners trample toward the exit, dealing @Damage[(2d10+9)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each living creature in area K9 and the hallway north of it (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature's grasp on anything it's carrying is weakened as if the hazard succeeded on a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} attempt. If its grasp was already weakened, they drop the item.

Special If the PCs have not defeated the hazard after 4 rounds, it automatically ends as all the prisoners escape.
Prison Break.name: Prison Break

Ravenous Pit.description: A seemingly ordinary hole in the forest floor exerts a strange gravity on creatures that come near.
Ravenous Pit.hazardDisable: @Check[nature|dc:23] or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (expert) three times to disperse the unusual energy surrounding the hole, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the engulfing magic
Ravenous Pit.hazardReset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures within 20 feet of it at the start of its turn.
Ravenous Pit.hazardRoutine:
(2 actions) For each action, the pit chooses a different creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] to attempt to swallow. That creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save. On a failure, the creature is pulled 5 feet closer to the hole (10 feet on a critical failure). If this would pull the creature into the pit, that creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} as a reaction (DC 22). A creature who falls into the pit disappears into an extradimensional space that feels like being buried alive. A creature takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save) at the end of each its turns in the extradimensional space. A creature in this extradimensional space is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}; they can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} this extradimensional space with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:22], Nature, or Survival check. If they succeed, they appear in a random spot no more than 10 feet from the hole as if they had clawed their way from the dirt.

While this trap is active, all creatures treat movement away from the pit as difficult terrain.
Ravenous Pit.name: Ravenous Pit

Redcap.description:
Redcaps are sadistic and capricious fey who thrill in bloodletting and murder. While they are most famous for appearing as wizened, bearded men, redcaps of other genders are no less fearsome or cruel. However, redcaps are ultimately craven bullies, cowed by anything more powerful than themselves, a trait that leads them to fear and despise the symbols of deities.

Many fairy tales explain how the redcaps draw power from dipping the hats for which they are named in fresh blood. Just as iconic to these cruel little fey are their iron-clad boots, and the clanging sound of their metal soles clanking on stone floors is both discordant and disconcerting—especially to those who recognize the sound for what it is. Redcaps typically stand only 3 feet tall, with hunched frames, pointed ears, crooked teeth, and long, white, tangled hair.

Though unlikely to share true affection, redcaps find camaraderie in murder. Small troupes of redcaps travel together, sharing bloody kills and reminiscing on their brutal exploits. They loathe the company of most other creatures, with the notable exception of the feline fey called elananxes.

2OPayJrYVYfP1LZk.description:
A redcap's woolen hat is dyed with the blood of their victims. If the redcap loses their cap, they no longer benefit from fast healing and take a –4 status penalty to their damage rolls. They can create a new cap in 10 minutes, but that cap doesn't grant them powers until the redcap has turned it red with Blood Soak. A cap has no benefit for creatures other than the redcap who made it.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.s5mS7CyE0oOYlecv]{Effect: Lost Red Cap}
2OPayJrYVYfP1LZk.name: Red Cap

7XdX39vruCh59MIe.description:
If a redcap sees a creature brandish a religious symbol of a deity (which requires an Interact action by that creature) or cast a divine spell while wearing a religious symbol, the redcap must attempt a @Check[will|dc:19] save. They then become temporarily immune to all brandished religious symbols for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The redcap is unaffected.

Success The redcap is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The redcap gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition for 1 round and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4}.
7XdX39vruCh59MIe.name: Divine Revulsion

P7S7nuGr7qWkygVT.description: The redcap Strides up to half its Speed and makes a boot Strike at any point during that movement. If the boot Strike hits a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} creature, it deals an extra @Damage[2d6[bleed]].
P7S7nuGr7qWkygVT.name: Stomp

Q9TQFtMzhVqywWrB.description:
Trigger The redcap reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points with a halberd Strike

Effect The redcap makes another halberd Strike against a different creature, using the same multiple attack penalty as the halberd Strike that triggered this reaction. This counts toward their multiple attack penalty as normal.
Q9TQFtMzhVqywWrB.name: Deadly Cleave

UZGw67PytFzgRdFP.description:
Whenever the redcap successfully Strikes a creature, that creature must attempt a Fortitude save against this debilitating fungal infection that slowly consumes a limb

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}, and using the infected limb deals @Damage[3d6[persistent,bleed]] damage (1 day)

Stage 3 clumsy 2, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}, and the infected limb is unusable (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 3}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3}, and infected limb is permanently unusable (1 day)

Stage 5 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused}, and damage does not end the confused condition (1 day)

Stage 6 death
UZGw67PytFzgRdFP.name: Red Drip

ivsgBmmJjZW5Wr5S.description:
The redcap dips their cap in the blood of a slain foe. The foe must have died in the last minute, and the redcap must have helped kill it. The redcap gains a +4 status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.wPIGBcpCqCWgrCiq]{Effect: Blood Soak}
ivsgBmmJjZW5Wr5S.name: Blood Soak

Redcap.name: Redcap

Ritual Backlash.description: The space above the flame in the ivory bowl wavers and the smell of brimstone assaults the senses.
Ritual Backlash.hazardDisable: @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] three times to suppress the backlash of energy, or defeat Viro Ahala
Ritual Backlash.hazardReset: Once Viro Ahala has been defeated, this trap can't be reset.
Ritual Backlash.hazardRoutine: (3 actions) The hazard makes a ranged mindburn Strike, creates a Pulse of Fear, and bestows Vitality of Hell to Viro or one of his allies at random. If it's reduced to 2 actions, the hazard doesn't create a Pulse of Fear. If reduced to 1 action, the hazard only bestows Vitality of Hell.
Ritual Backlash.name: Ritual Backlash

Sarcovalt.description:
Individual sarcovalts have almost no personal identities and incredibly short-term memories.

There are few creatures throughout the multiverse more bizarrely revolting than the sarcovalt, a fiend with the body of a fly and the head of a fleshless vulture skull. While a creature barely 2 feet long might not seem threatening, sarcovalt swarms can overwhelm entire villages. Sarcovalts' heads almost seem to be wholly separate entities from their bodies and will vomit up whatever they capture in their beaks hours later as putrid, coagulated sludge.

AtCXVdtehljc8QdT.description:
Trigger The sarcovalt takes slashing damage from a creature while they have another creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The sarcovalt uses Detach Head. Their head keeps the other creature grabbed or restrained, and their body Steps. The sarcovalt gains resistance 5 to the triggering slashing damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.78Dfh5ghvULGiyzk]{Effect: Reactive Detach}
AtCXVdtehljc8QdT.name: Reactive Detach

FO3rk85tKmhFRdrU.description:
Trigger The sarcovalt takes damage from a critical hit with a slashing weapon or has their head removed (such as by a vorpal weapon)

Effect The sarcovalt can survive decapitation and isn't killed if an effect that removes their head would kill them. Instead, the sarcovalt's head becomes detached from their body. The detached head has the same statistics as the body (except for the aura of blight) and gains the minion trait. The sarcovalt's body can issue a verbal command to the detached head as a single action, giving the head 2 actions. The detached head and body share the same pool of Hit Points.

While their head is detached, the sarcovalt's body can't make beak Strikes or use Malodorous Shriek. The detached head can only Climb, Fly, Stride, make beak Strikes, or use Malodorous Shriek.
FO3rk85tKmhFRdrU.name: Detach Head

KWvn4JKkgo0whAiV.description: The sarcovalt screeches through their skull (detached or not), spraying whatever festering viscera remains in their head in a @Template[type:cone|distance:20]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[3d6[sonic],3d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). The sarcovalt can't use this ability again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge this ability]]{1d4 rounds}.
KWvn4JKkgo0whAiV.name: Malodorous Shriek

aqLTyLAwckInoRjA.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Death Effects

nZFgHZXnO78HnnPP.description:
Any water the sarcovalt touches becomes diseased with filth fever.

Filth Fever (disease)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:23|traits:disease]

Onset [[/br 1d4 #days]]{1d4 days}

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 day)
nZFgHZXnO78HnnPP.name: Befouled Body

qYjVntqomH6GMQGX.description:
15 feet. The sarcovalt is surrounded by a miasma of infection. An enemy that enters or ends their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save.

Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 minute.

Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saves against disease effects.

Critical Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –2 circumstance bonus against disease effects and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}.
qYjVntqomH6GMQGX.name: Aura of Blight

xBRFeBqx4UcT38rV.description:
Trigger A creature escapes from being @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the sarcovalt's detached head

Effect The sarcovalt's head returns to their body via the shortest possible path. Any creature in a straight line between the head and the body must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical failure). The sarcovalt Dismisses Detach Head.
xBRFeBqx4UcT38rV.name: Return to Sender

Sarcovalt.name: Sarcovalt
Sarcovalt.npcAllSaves: +1 status to all saves vs. death effects

Sarcovalt Swarm.description:
In most cases, it's very unlikely that one will come across a sarcovalt on their own. More likely, a swarm of Urgathoa's disgusting creations will be beset upon an unfortunate village, the creatures just clever enough to duck away from the best attempts to kill them.

There are few creatures throughout the multiverse more bizarrely revolting than the sarcovalt, a fiend with the body of a fly and the head of a fleshless vulture skull. While a creature barely 2 feet long might not seem threatening, sarcovalt swarms can overwhelm entire villages. Sarcovalts' heads almost seem to be wholly separate entities from their bodies and will vomit up whatever they capture in their beaks hours later as putrid, coagulated sludge.

4dOF92ZHlnfvcC4d.description:
15 feet. The sarcovalt is surrounded by a miasma of infection. An enemy that enters or ends their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save.

Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 minute.

Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saves against disease effects.

Critical Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –2 circumstance bonus against disease effects and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2}.
4dOF92ZHlnfvcC4d.name: Aura of Blight

5hZxx4bVaqMR8rps.description: The swarm detaches its heads from its bodies and rains them down on its enemies in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] radius within 30 feet. Each head deals falling damage, as if falling from 20 feet up, on any creature it lands on and otherwise takes the damage itself. If a head has a creature within its reach after it falls, it attempts to bite that creature. If any of these bites are critical successes, the head can immediately attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapple} the creature.
5hZxx4bVaqMR8rps.name: Heads Will Roll

9tXYasHPuZ7tB7bo.description: The buzzing of the swarm's wings hit an unholy pitch, making those who hear it feel dizzy and lightheaded. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15] must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save. On a failure, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} and takes a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls until the start of its next turn (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure).
9tXYasHPuZ7tB7bo.name: Droning Waste

MCdYvumxzVh8yf22.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Death Effects

TjyddrbQCGwox9Im.description: Each enemy in the swarm's space takes @Damage[4d6[piercing],2d4[persistent,bleed]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:27|basic] save).
TjyddrbQCGwox9Im.name: Swarming Bites

Sarcovalt Swarm.name: Sarcovalt Swarm
Sarcovalt Swarm.npcAllSaves: +1 status to all saves vs. death effects

2D5BEXHCiTqeMs3D.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect Sephone drains her religious symbol and regains a spell she has cast. During her current turn, she can cast one spell she prepared today and already cast.
2D5BEXHCiTqeMs3D.name: Drain Religious Symbol

KVJRqYVIfgP9zys7.description:
Frequency three times per day

Effect Sephone fuels her spell with the essence of Hell. The next spell she casts deals fire damage instead of its usual damage type. Any creature that takes damage from the spell and doesn't have the unholy trait also takes an additional @Damage[2d8[spirit]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save).
KVJRqYVIfgP9zys7.name: Channel Hellfire

bPpVRdWyfTzOe767.description:
Trigger A creature Casts a Spell Sephone has prepared

Effect Sephone expends a prepared spell, losing the spell slot as if she had cast the triggering spell. She then attempts to counteract the triggering spell.
bPpVRdWyfTzOe767.name: Counterspell

g60IZKVskFvCnLHe.description: If the next action Sephone uses is to Cast a Spell that has an area of a cone or line and does not have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
g60IZKVskFvCnLHe.name: Widen Spell

2M47ZIiGcz70JQp6.description: Sister Endreah makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.
2M47ZIiGcz70JQp6.name: Flurry of Blows

U5wiezmGzheE4kq9.description: When Sister Endreah targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows, she can cause lingering pain. If either Strike hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save or take @Damage[1d4[persistent,mental]] damage (and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a critical failure).
U5wiezmGzheE4kq9.name: Claw of Pain

jeyApF42KCXJlY4v.description:
Requirements Sister Endreah is wielding an asp coil

Effect Sister Endreah is an expert at using her sword in close quarters. Her asp coil loses the reach weapon property, but she can make asp coil Strikes instead of fist Strikes when she uses Flurry of Blows. Claw of pain also applies to these asp coil Strikes when in this stance.
jeyApF42KCXJlY4v.name: Bite of the Asp Stance

K59aGsMWBm5sefJ8.description: When the sister targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows, she can cause lingering pain. If either Strike hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save or take @Damage[1d4[persistent,mental]] damage (and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a critical failure).
K59aGsMWBm5sefJ8.name: Claw of Pain

LawkCdKD2sGuJ2XG.description: The sister makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.
LawkCdKD2sGuJ2XG.name: Flurry of Blows

Skeletal Hellknight Bonesinger.description:
Some undead Hellknights can command the bones of both their own bodies and others, and they primarily use this ability to temporarily strengthen themselves and summon waves of reinforcements. While just as undead as their compatriots, these so-called bonesingers have enough intelligence to join their living counterparts, though they're generally shunned and disavowed.

The bodies of Hellknights occasionally rise to continue their fight. This is just one example of an undead soldier of Hell.

0yUaKsY0wVM8j01L.description: The skeletal Hellknight bonesinger strikes its rib cage three times, creating a loud, ominous clatter. All mindless skeletons in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must use their reaction to Stride closer to the bonesinger.
0yUaKsY0wVM8j01L.name: Call to Arms

FSEozSpG3gJKBYjt.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements The skeletal Hellknight bonesinger's last action was to damage an enemy with a melee Strike

Effect The bonesinger pulls at the bones of the creature they just damaged, releasing a pulse of void energy. That creature takes @Damage[4d8[void]] damage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.
FSEozSpG3gJKBYjt.name: Invoke Reckoning

oUSZl1Y3qSKGLTdw.description:
Frequency once per day

Requirements The skeletal Hellknight bonesinger has fewer than half their Hit Points remaining

Effect The bonesinger reaches out through the necromantic magic that animates the dead around them and pulls it into themself. The bonesinger chooses up to three mindless skeletons with the minion trait that they control within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] and destroys them. The bonesinger regains 2d6 Hit Points for each skeleton destroyed.
oUSZl1Y3qSKGLTdw.name: For the Cause

Skeletal Hellknight Bonesinger.name: Skeletal Hellknight Bonesinger

Skirmancer Mage.description:
A watchmage uses a mix of magic and martial training to enforce the law. They magically detect invisible criminals, locate stolen property, and counter illegal spells.

Larger societies rely on those with the authority and the ability to interpret and enforce laws. Some carry out these duties fairly, but others are harsh and cruel, imposing severe punishments on anyone unable to pay for clemency.

6sz1UfpfaZl4UW3Z.description:
Trigger A creature Casts a Spell with the teleportation trait or as a reaction

Effect The watchmage expends a spell slot of the same rank or higher as the trigger creature's spell and attempts to counteract the triggering spell (counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+11 #Counteract]]{+11}).
6sz1UfpfaZl4UW3Z.name: Counter Escape

7mleAGFfBWpFQl7d.description: In a brief ritual that takes 10 minutes, the watchmage chooses a single weapon or unarmed attack through which they can focus their magic. Strikes the watchmage makes with that weapon are magical and deal 1d6 additional force damage.
7mleAGFfBWpFQl7d.name: Eldritch Arms

I49qWF8BgMJ9IdLd.description:
Contains all prepared spells plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gPvtmKMRpg9I9D7H]{Earthbind}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.0qaqksrGGDj74HXE]{Revealing Light}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.o4lRVTwSxnOOn5vl]{Sleep}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KcLVELhCUcKXxiKE]{Tailwind}.

A spellbook holds the written knowledge necessary to learn and prepare various spells, a necessity for wizards (who typically get one for free) and a useful luxury for other spellcasters looking to learn additional spells. Each spellbook can hold up to 100 spells. The Price listed is for a blank spellbook.
I49qWF8BgMJ9IdLd.name: Spellbook

gcs26pIHID48TUdP.description: The watchmage can either @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EwgTZBWsc8qKaViP]{Investigate} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.TiNDYUGlMmxzxBYU]{Search} while using the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Yb0C1uLzeHrVLl7a]{Detect Magic} exploration activity.
gcs26pIHID48TUdP.name: Arcane Watch

hTuNAi9LMU6sxtte.description:
Requirements The watchmage is wielding the weapon chosen with Eldritch Arms

Effect The watchmage Casts a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast, imbuing that spell into the weapon. The watchmage Strikes with the required weapon. This counts as two attacks for the watchmage's multiple attack penalty. On a hit, the target is also affected by the spell, though the target gets any normal defenses allowed by the spell.

If the spell is targeted, it targets the creature that was hit and no one else. If the spell is an area, the target must be in the area. A burst is centered on a corner of the target's square if the target is Medium or smaller, or the corner of a square closest to the creature's center if it's Large or larger. A cone or line emits from a square of the watchmage's choice adjacent to the target.
hTuNAi9LMU6sxtte.name: Spellbound Strike

Skirmancer Mage.name: Skirmancer Mage

Slashing Rune.description: An invisible rune creates knives of force if someone tries to bypass the lock.
Slashing Rune.hazardDisable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to silently erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the rune
Slashing Rune.name: Slashing Rune

Spore Spears.description: A large patch of moss ripples like the surface of a pond as spears of green plant matter jut outward.
Spore Spears.hazardDisable: @Check[nature|dc:17] or @Check[occultism|dc:17] twice to cut off the unstable connection to the Plane of Wood, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (2nd rank; counteract DC 16) to counteract the planar magic
Spore Spears.hazardReset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures on or within 10 feet of the moss patch at the start of its turn.
Spore Spears.hazardRoutine: (2 actions) For each action, the moss pond makes a spore spear Strike against a random non-plant creature on or within 10 feet of the moss pond.
Spore Spears.name: Spore Spears

Spreading Fire.description: A fire engulfs a 5-foot-by-5-foot area, then spreads on each of its turns.
Spreading Fire.hazardDisable: @Check[athletics|dc:17] to splash the barrels of gall ink on the fire or @Check[survival|dc:15] to smother the flames
Spreading Fire.hazardRoutine: (1 action) On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies, with a minimum of 1 square. You determine the squares the fire spreads into—typically those with the most flammable materials. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage. Both of these have a @Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save. A creature can take damage from flames only once per round.
Spreading Fire.name: Spreading Fire

8R6lMXORDkYxqgM3.description: The web of the empress katroome wraps creatures into a cocoon. The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} while it's enveloped in a cocoon ([[/act escape dc=26]]). The web has Hardness 10 and 20 Hit Points. It has resistance 5 to bludgeoning and piercing and weakness 5 to slashing. At the end of the target's turn, if it has yet to Escape from enveloping web, the web grows, increasing the slowed value by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 3}. The katroome can envelop up to three creatures at a time.
8R6lMXORDkYxqgM3.name: Enveloping Web

R8BUg5bOgzUduBD2.description: 40 feet. All creatures with the fey trait who enter or begin their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature becomes immune to the aura for 1 day. On a failure, the creature can't take hostile actions against the katroome.
R8BUg5bOgzUduBD2.name: Pacify Fey

SAvHN1viZsrXMnxM.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The katroome flaps its wings of prismatic scales. All creatures within a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] takes @Damage[9d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save. On a failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} on a critical failure.
SAvHN1viZsrXMnxM.name: Glittering Scales

Stair Spikes.description: A set of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} spikes stabs into the feet of anyone on the landing or front stairs.
Stair Spikes.hazardDisable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to unhook the pressure plate or @Check[crafting|dc:24] (trained) to block the spikes
Stair Spikes.name: Stair Spikes

Stumpfield War Chanter Choir.description: The goblin artists of Stumpfield love a good song, as most goblins do. While they aren't primarily warriors, these goblins have taken up their dogslicers to fight for the liberation of Isger. Their main advantage as a troop asset in skirmish encounters, though, is their ability to bolster their allies and flummox their enemies with their chants.

CYb0q1kEp48dd5fd.description: 30 feet. The war chanters constantly sing and cheer to spur their Hellbreaker allies. Each allied creature in the aura gains a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against mental effects.
CYb0q1kEp48dd5fd.name: In Our Wake, Hell Will Break!

TL0WviWoCX05NGwo.description: The war chanters break out into a familiar song with unfamiliar rhyme. A number of enemies equal to the troop's current number of segments within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save. Regardless of the outcome, a target becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 1 minute. On a failure, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round as they get distracted by the war chanters' rhymes (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure).
TL0WviWoCX05NGwo.name: Hark! The Goblins Chanters Sing!

aYCK7nTLUcZDQWtM.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The war chanters swing their dogslicers, attacking each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+2)[slashing]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+8)[slashing]] damage

3 @Damage[(3d6+11)[slashing]] damage
aYCK7nTLUcZDQWtM.name: Goblins Slice and Goblins Dice!

Stumpfield War Chanter Choir.name: Stumpfield War Chanter Choir

Stumpfield War Saboteurs.description: Stumpfield's artists enjoy the chaos of creation, which makes them well suited for the parallel chaos of destruction. Isn't sabotage just another form of art? Stumpfield certainly thinks so, and its denizens are happy to use their creative skills to waylay enemies and ambush parties.

2dUzY0p9gwSx5I7C.description:
Frequency once per round;

Effect The saboteurs engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[1d8[slashing]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+5)[slashing]] damage

3 @Damage[(2d8+9)[slashing]] damage
2dUzY0p9gwSx5I7C.name: Slice and Dice!

SzGWtFMZlNoEwZaz.description:
Requirements The saboteurs' last action was to successfully Hide

Effect The saboteurs laid down falling obstacles as they move unseen. The saboteurs @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneak} twice. Until the beginning of the saboteurs' next turn, any non-ally creature that moves into a square that the saboteurs snuck through takes @Damage[5d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. Once this trap has been triggered, the rest of the squares the saboteurs snuck through become difficult terrain until a creature spends 1 hour clearing it.
SzGWtFMZlNoEwZaz.name: Slapstick Traps

eP5o4KaLed0eEmWX.description:
Trigger The saboteurs are targeted by a Strike or an effect that requires a Reflex save

Effect The saboteurs scramble for the nearest cover. They become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} but gain @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.other-effects.Item.I9lfZUiCwMiGogVi]{Standard Cover} until the beginning of their next turn.
eP5o4KaLed0eEmWX.name: Duck, Duck, Loose!

n9RpO5CMZ9E4B8wI.description: Quickly moving in with grasping hands, the Stumpfield war saboteurs take what they can. The saboteurs Stride up to twice their Speed. During this movement, the saboteurs Interact to pick up an unattended object no larger than 2 Bulk or attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.RDXXE7wMrSPCLv5k]{Steal} an item from a creature they're adjacent to; the goblins can pick up or Steal as many objects as they have remaining segments in any combination.
n9RpO5CMZ9E4B8wI.name: Rush and Steal

Stumpfield War Saboteurs.name: Stumpfield War Saboteurs

Swiftrun Clergy.description:
Swiftrun is a small settlement dedicated to Erastil, barely large enough to be considered a village. Its priests are devoted to community and peace, nature and nurture. And yet, sometimes the time comes when one must take up arms to protect their community, when words and gentleness aren't enough.

The Erastilian clergy is known more for their farming techniques than their magic, but their rural ways shouldn't fool anyone into thinking they can't defend themselves. Each priest is equipped with the divine power of Old Deadeye as well as sharpshooting skills conducive to Erastil's favored weapon, the longbow. These priests are prepared to defend their homes with their skills when they must, though they are never eager to shed blood and would rather spend their days tending their crops and community.

2WyuFPxG4SdahXgx.description: The Swiftrun clergy draw their longbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|traits:area-damage] save. When the regiment is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].
2WyuFPxG4SdahXgx.name: Longbow Volley

PVYcvavRgEgDbplK.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Swiftrun's clergy swiftly strike with their daggers against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d4+1)[piercing]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d4+7)[piercing]] damage

3 @Damage[(2d4+10)[piercing]] damage
PVYcvavRgEgDbplK.name: Swift Daggers

bHkMNtWxWD8ma7vh.description: When the Swiftrun clergy Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
bHkMNtWxWD8ma7vh.name: Troop Spellcasting

Swiftrun Clergy.name: Swiftrun Clergy

Thin Ice.description: The white-and-blue ice is less structurally sound than it looks.
Thin Ice.hazardDisable: @Check[nature|dc:31] or @Check[survival|dc:31] (expert) to spread out and prevent the ice from cracking further, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:31] or @Check[athletics|dc:31] (trained) to get off the ice safely as it cracks.
Thin Ice.hazardReset: The river freezes over every night, restoring this hazard. The hazard disappears once the vernal equinox passes.
Thin Ice.name: Thin Ice

Umok Beastspeaker Circle.description:
The gnomes of Umok have a reticence to fire after the pyrrhic wildfire that razed the Chitterwood during the Goblinblood Wars. To help protect their home from unwanted arson, many gnomes of Umok have learned to communicate with animals, whose instincts can quickly sniff out unauthorized smoke or flame. The two groups work together to keep the town safe, with the animals serving as an early-warning system when threats approach.

Several Umok beastspeakers have joined the war effort, harnessing their primal magic and their connection with animals against the armies of Cheliax. Their powerful magics are used to summon beasts of the wild to defend Isger from forces that threaten the peace of their home, pitting primal power against fiends.

OYc5E5k1PKyHFqeL.description: When the beastspeaker circle Casts a Spell, the individual members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
OYc5E5k1PKyHFqeL.name: Troop Spellcasting

PoCHXLvk6Nt9a5GS.description: The beastspeakers summon a group of wild animals to attack all creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet. The animals deal @Damage[3d8[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). When the beastspeaker circle is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].
PoCHXLvk6Nt9a5GS.name: Muster Animals

gqVWmn4vvhygueaQ.description: The beastspeaker circle can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals.
gqVWmn4vvhygueaQ.name: Animal Empathy

kP9VOFgnYBxtGrK6.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The beastspeakers grip their staves with two hands and attack all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save. The amount of damage depends on the number of actions used.

1 @Damage[(1d8+1)[bludgeoning]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+6)[bludgeoning]] damage

3 @Damage[(2d8+10)[bludgeoning]] damage
kP9VOFgnYBxtGrK6.name: Staff Assault

Umok Beastspeaker Circle.name: Umok Beastspeaker Circle

4r1v6qnOQPxNqVwr.description:
Frequency once per minute

Trigger A creature attempts a flat check to target Varlario while they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} due to dim light or darkness

Effect Shadows wrap around Varlario's form. The DC of the flat check increases by 2.
4r1v6qnOQPxNqVwr.name: Shadow Blending

QDMXH5o8ftWutjTp.description: Varlario has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} their bat familiar through the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.F1nlmqOIucch3Cmt]{Pet Cache} spell. They keep the bat contained except when they use shred spirit.
QDMXH5o8ftWutjTp.name: Hidden Familiar

xNrqFLKceXzkzUGF.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect Varlario sends forth their bat familiar as a phantasmal conduit to rip at the soul of a creature within 30 feet. The targeted creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:26|basic] save or take @Damage[9d6[spirit]] damage. Varlario then recovers Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt.
xNrqFLKceXzkzUGF.name: Shred Spirit

Velavi.description:
Knowledge is power, and no one is more keenly aware of this truth than velavis. These scribes and junior scholars are tasked with maintaining the unfathomably vast collections in Stygia's Academies of Lies. Velavis are the front faces of the library, greeting visitors and determining whether to grant their requests. Their organizational skills are among the best in Hell, and their memories are long, allowing them to easily navigate their home library with astonishing speed. Velavis provide another vital service for Hell: should another devil make a valuable discovery, they're expected to report to an Academy to have a velavi record it on a scroll or book the velavi crafts out of living flesh.

Each velavi is intimately familiar with the collections in their library, and they have unfettered access to other Academies' archives. Those seeking information in Hell might be tempted to seek a velavi's aid, but the velavi will demand a high price before conceding any information—even then, the flow of knowledge almost always goes one way, as velavis are tricky with what they share. Librarian devils are custodians, gatekeepers, and guardians; it's exceedingly rare for velavis to share their knowledge with anyone but a devil of higher rank, and even then, it must be bought or threatened out of them.

VMXkzlTFynhqIrxo.description:
The velavi makes an intimidating shushing motion. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. The velavi can't use Frightening Shush again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Frightening Shush]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}. While a creature is frightened by this ability, it's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the velavi.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature also can't make any sounds while it's frightened, as if under the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gIdDLrbswTV3OBJy]{Silence} spell.
VMXkzlTFynhqIrxo.name: Frightening Shush

jSpNbVe0BjRlGRO9.description:
@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xqmHD8JIjak15lRk]{Phantasmal Minion}

Casting a Spell from a scroll requires holding the scroll in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell.

The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell also gains the appropriate trait for your tradition (arcane, divine, occult, or primal).

Any physical costs are provided when a scroll is created, so you don't need to provide them when casting from a scroll. If the spell requires a locus, you must have that locus to Cast the Spell from a scroll.

Note: To create a scroll or wand of a specific spell, drag the spell from the compendium or compendium browser into the inventory of a PC, NPC, or loot actor.
jSpNbVe0BjRlGRO9.name: Scroll of Phantasmal Minion (Rank 1) (Scroll Robes)

Warshard Wight.description:
Wights are intelligent undead spawned through inescapable cycles of spite. This spite might come from their own malevolent will in life, or can be instilled by necromantic rituals, typically involving the desecration of burial sites. Wights usually haunt burial grounds, catacombs, or other places of the dead. Their hunger is targeted toward the living—those whose pumping hearts and ruddy warmth inspire visceral hatred.

As many types of wights exist as types of people from which they might be created. Hulking brutes, skittering sneaks, and cunning tinkers all make for different wights with different niches to fill. The environment, too, plays a part in determining a wight's special abilities and defenses. Frost wights, for instance, can be found in parts of the world where exposure is a common end and the resentment of being left alone in the wild is a common source of spite. Durable and sustained by void energy, wights can last in harsh environments without decaying the way some lesser undead do.

A single wight can wreak significant havoc if it is compelled to rise from its tomb. Because creatures slain under a wight's curse can become wights as well, all it takes is a single wight and a handful of unlucky graveyard visitors to create a veritable horde of these undead. Thus, canny priests and adventurers know that the best solution to a wight problem is swift and total eradication. Care must be taken, though, to destroy wight spawn before attempting to destroy the parent wight, for spawn without a master gain the ability to create spawn of their own.

16ZFULnotAOQsWlX.description:
The warshard wight cries out in an unearthly shriek. Living creatures within 50 feet must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save. The warshard wight can't make another bloody wail for [[/br 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature bleeds from their ears and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature bleeds from their ears and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature takes a –2 status penalty to saving throws against drain life.
16ZFULnotAOQsWlX.name: Bloody Wail

Kpl3lLjfHM0hWhT5.description:
Trigger The warshward wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The warshard wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. It doesn't gain any temporary Hit Points from drain life on this Strike.
Kpl3lLjfHM0hWhT5.name: Final Spite

eR3sH9CYaDBXiida.description: The warshard wight has a scythe arm resembling a Child of Urgathoa.
eR3sH9CYaDBXiida.name: Urgathoa's Weapon

qV4pHys0UrAOFtPw.description: A living humanoid slain by a warshard wight's scything blade or claw Strike rises as a spawned wight after [[/br 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. This spawned wight is under the command of the warshard wight that killed it. It doesn't have drain life or warshard wight spawn and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for as long as it's a spawned wight. If its creator dies, the spawned wight becomes a full-fledged, autonomous warshard wight; it regains its free will, gains drain life and warshard wight spawn, and is no longer clumsy.
qV4pHys0UrAOFtPw.name: Warshard Wight Spawn

s11JB7gJU2rA1XmT.description: When the warshard wight damages a living creature with a melee Strike using an unarmed attack or its bound weapon, the warshard wight gains 5 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}. Further damage dealt by the warshard wight increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4}.
s11JB7gJU2rA1XmT.name: Drain Life

Warshard Wight.name: Warshard Wight

Zombie Giant Bat.description:
While big bats are certainly not uncommon in dark caves and abandoned ruins and may instill fear in squeamish spelunkers, the so-called giant bat is a true monster, weighing well over 100 pounds and having a wingspan of nearly 15 feet. It primarily eats fruit and bugs, but can be incited to violence through fear or hunger. Giant bat attacks can quickly give rise to rumors of more dangerous monsters—many mistake these massive animals for some sort of demon or vampiric monster. But like other bats, giant bats are simply social and intelligent mammals. They are sometimes used as mounts by smaller humanoids, commonly those who hail from or dwell in mountainous or underground regions.

A wide range of bats dwell throughout the world. Most of these nocturnal animals are harmless insectivores, but deadly breeds of vampire bats and oversized bats the size of horses pose much more significant threats to adventurers.

1q30WvWn7CmDC7GI.description: While a zombie giant bat can fly, its wings are barely able to keep it aloft. If it's airborne when it takes a critical hit or any amount of slashing damage, it falls and it can't Fly on its next turn.
1q30WvWn7CmDC7GI.name: Clumsy Flier

8cb0jB7NP8JMjdkx.description: A zombie is permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use reactions.
8cb0jB7NP8JMjdkx.name: Slow

rFVy8L2CfrTjRtZf.description: 10 feet. The zombie giant bat emits an aura of rot and disease that cause wounds to fester. Any living creature that starts its turn within the aura and isn't at full Hit Points takes @Damage[1d6[void]] damage.
rFVy8L2CfrTjRtZf.name: Rotting Aura
